title: Tanker Report - Week 39 2015
summary:
VLCCs
A steady week for VLCC owners with the market holding at around WS 64/65 level basis 270,000 tonnes for long runs east from the Middle East Gulf. Charterers able or willing to take tonnage over 15 years old and secure significant discounts were evident, with the Millenium 1998 built fixing to FPCC at WS 54 for Taiwan discharge. Going west, Valero was said to have covered 280,000 tonnes to the US Gulf at WS 37.5 with trips to UK-Cont reportedly done at WS 39 basis Suez/Suez routing.

children.title: VLCCs
children.summary: A steady week for VLCC owners with the market holding at around WS 64/65 level basis 270,000 tonnes for long runs east from the Middle East Gulf. Charterers able or willing to take tonnage over 15 years old and secure significant discounts were evident, with the Millenium 1998 built fixing to FPCC at WS 54 for Taiwan discharge.

children.title: Suezmaxes
children.summary: A very active week in West Africa saw rates rise now around 25 points to close to WS 100 level. Replacement and requoted cargoes, including VLCC cargoes being split into suezmax stems helped owners build momentum.

children.title: Aframaxes
children.summary: Another disappointing week for owners with prompt tonnage in the Mediterranean as rates dip further to mid-high WS 60s for both east Mediterranean and Black Sea loading. Owners looked to a firmer suezmax market resulting in stem splitting benefitting aframaxes, but this needed to be combined with a significant clear out of ships.

children.title: Panamaxes
children.summary: Rates for Continent tonnage remained largely unchanged with enquiry matched by available tonnage with rates at WS 87.5/90 for 55,000 tonnes from ARA to the US Gulf.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: A volatile week in the market for 37,000 tonnes from Continent to USAC with slipping WS 100 at the start of the week. Charterers paid 10 points more for replacement tonnage and a non-replacement at the same rate but eased have eased back.
